page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter and Starlite Festival: Evening Cruise Tips

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella yacht charter for Starlite Festival 2025. Evening cruise tips, departure from Puerto Banús, and boat options from €749 for 8 guests.
data.summary: Combine a Marbella yacht charter with the Starlite Festival for an unforgettable evening. This guide covers departure tips, boat options, and how to make the most of your night.
key_facts: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, 10 minutes from Marbella Marina.
Starlite Festival runs June–August in the Nagüeles quarry, Marbella.
Evening cruises start from €749/2h (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
Boats include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA.
Mangusta 80 (24m) available from €4,719/4h with free jet ski.
Book early: festival tickets sell out weeks in advance.
Cruise before the concert: sunset over La Concha is spectacular.

faq.q: How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is about 10 minutes by taxi or private transfer from the Nagüeles quarry where Starlite is held. Most charters include a return to the marina, so you can easily dock and head to the concert.

faq.q: Can I combine a yacht charter with a Starlite Festival evening?
faq.a: Absolutely. Many guests book a 2-hour sunset cruise (€749) before the concert, or an 8-hour charter (€2,299) for a full day on the water followed by the festival. We recommend departing by 6 PM to enjoy the coast before the show.

faq.q: What drinks are included on the yacht charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. You can bring your own food or arrange catering for an extra fee.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a Starlite evening cruise?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are popular for smaller groups at €749/2h. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers luxury and a free jet ski from €4,719/4h.

faq.q: Do I need to book the yacht charter in advance?
faq.a: Yes, especially during the Starlite Festival season (June–August). Boats book up quickly, so we recommend reserving at least 2–3 weeks ahead. Contact us via WhatsApp for availability.

faq.q: What time should I depart for a Starlite evening?
faq.a: For a sunset cruise, depart Puerto Banús around 6 PM. This gives you 2 hours to enjoy the coast, watch the sunset over La Concha, and arrive back at the marina by 8 PM to head to the festival, which typically starts at 9 PM.

faq.q: Is the yacht charter suitable for a pre-festival party?
faq.a: Yes, many groups use the charter as a pre-party. The boats have sound systems, and you can bring your own playlist. Just keep noise levels reasonable out of respect for other mariners.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day?
faq.a: We monitor conditions closely. If a charter is cancelled due to weather, you receive a full refund or can reschedule. Most evenings in Marbella are calm, especially in summer.

data.body_html:
The Starlite Festival in Marbella is one of Europe's most glamorous summer events, drawing top artists and celebrities to the Nagüeles quarry. Combining a Marbella yacht charter with a Starlite evening creates a seamless luxury experience: cruise the Costa del Sol at sunset, anchor off the Golden Mile, then head to the concert. This guide covers practical tips, boat options, and how to make the most of your night.

Why Combine a Yacht Charter with Starlite Festival?

Starlite concerts start around 9 PM, leaving you the entire afternoon and early evening free. A yacht charter from Puerto Banús lets you enjoy the Mediterranean before the show. You can cruise past the Marbella coastline, stop for a swim at Cala del Faro, and watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain. The charter includes drinks and snacks, so you arrive at the festival relaxed and refreshed.

Best Boats for a Starlite Evening Cruise

Our fleet offers several options depending on group size and budget. For couples or small groups (up to 9 guests), the Astondoa 40 (12.5m) or Azimut 39 (12.5m) are ideal at €749 for 2 hours (skipper, drinks, IVA included). For a longer day, the 8-hour charter at €2,299 allows you to explore Cabopino or Estepona before the concert. Larger groups (up to 12 guests) should consider the Mangusta 80 (24m) from €4,719 for 4 hours, which includes a free jet ski. All boats depart from Puerto Banús.

Typical Evening Itinerary

  • 6:00 PM: Board your yacht at Puerto Banús. Meet the skipper, enjoy welcome drinks.
  • 6:15 PM: Depart towards Marbella Marina. Cruise past the Golden Mile villas.
  • 6:45 PM: Anchor at Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro for a swim and sunset photos.
  • 7:30 PM: Sail back, enjoying cava and snacks as the sun sets.
  • 8:00 PM: Dock at Puerto Banús. Taxi to Starlite (10 minutes).

What's Included in Every Charter

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. You can bring your own food or arrange catering via WhatsApp.

Booking Tips for Starlite Festival

Starlite tickets sell out quickly, especially for headliners. Book your concert tickets first, then contact us for the yacht charter. We recommend reserving at least 2–3 weeks in advance during June–August. For the best sunset views, choose a 2-hour charter departing at 6 PM. If you want a full day on the water, the 8-hour option gives you time to visit Sotogrande or Estepona before the concert.

Local Knowledge: Best Sunset Spots

From Puerto Banús, the sunset over La Concha is iconic. For a quieter experience, head towards Cabopino or the dunes of Artola. The Mangusta 80 can anchor closer to shore, giving you uninterrupted views. If you're on the Azimut 58 Flybridge, the upper deck offers panoramic sightlines. Don't forget your camera—the light is golden from 7:30 PM onwards.

Frequently Asked Questions

How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?

Puerto Banús is about 10 minutes by taxi or private transfer from the Nagüeles quarry where Starlite is held. Most charters include a return to the marina, so you can easily dock and head to the concert.

Can I combine a yacht charter with a Starlite Festival evening?

Absolutely. Many guests book a 2-hour sunset cruise (€749) before the concert, or an 8-hour charter (€2,299) for a full day on the water followed by the festival. We recommend departing by 6 PM to enjoy the coast before the show.

What drinks are included on the yacht charter?

Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. You can bring your own food or arrange catering for an extra fee.

Which boats are best for a Starlite evening cruise?

The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are popular for smaller groups at €749/2h. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers luxury and a free jet ski from €4,719/4h.

Do I need to book the yacht charter in advance?

Yes, especially during the Starlite Festival season (June–August). Boats book up quickly, so we recommend reserving at least 2–3 weeks ahead. Contact us via WhatsApp for availability.

What time should I depart for a Starlite evening?

For a sunset cruise, depart Puerto Banús around 6 PM. This gives you 2 hours to enjoy the coast, watch the sunset over La Concha, and arrive back at the marina by 8 PM to head to the festival, which typically starts at 9 PM.

Is the yacht charter suitable for a pre-festival party?

Yes, many groups use the charter as a pre-party. The boats have sound systems, and you can bring your own playlist. Just keep noise levels reasonable out of respect for other mariners.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day?

We monitor conditions closely. If a charter is cancelled due to weather, you receive a full refund or can reschedule. Most evenings in Marbella are calm, especially in summer.
